CREATE TABLE list_test1 (list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO list_test1 VALUES (ARRAY[1, 2, 3], ARRAY[2, 3, 4]);
SELECT array_intersect(list1, list2) FROM list_test1;
CREATE TABLE list_test2 (list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO list_test2 VALUES (ARRAY[NULL, 1, 2], ARRAY[NULL, 2, 3]);
SELECT array_intersect(list1, list2) FROM list_test2;
CREATE TABLE list_test3 (id INTEGER, list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO list_test3 VALUES (1, ARRAY[1, 1, 2], ARRAY[1, 2, 3]);
SELECT id, array_intersect(list1, list2) FROM list_test3;
CREATE TABLE list_test4 (list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO list_test4 VALUES (ARRAY[1, 2, 3], ARRAY[]);
SELECT array_intersect(list1, list2) FROM list_test4;
CREATE TABLE list_test5 (id INTEGER, list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO list_test5 VALUES (1, ARRAY[1, 2, 3], ARRAY[2, 2, 3]), (2, ARRAY[4, 5], ARRAY[5, 6, 7]);
SELECT id, array_intersect(list1, list2) FROM list_test5;
CREATE TABLE nested_lists (list1 ARRAY<ARRAY<INTEGER>>, list2 ARRAY<ARRAY<INTEGER>>);
INSERT INTO nested_lists VALUES (ARRAY[ARRAY[1, 2], ARRAY[3]], ARRAY[ARRAY[2], ARRAY[3, 4]]);
SELECT array_intersect(list1[0], list2[0]) FROM nested_lists;
CREATE TABLE complex_lists (id INTEGER, list1 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list2 ARRAY<INTEGER>, list3 ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO complex_lists VALUES (1, ARRAY[1, 2], ARRAY[2, 3], ARRAY[1, 3]), (2, ARRAY[5, 6], ARRAY[6, 7], ARRAY[5, 8]);
SELECT id, array_intersect(array_intersect(list1, list2), list3) FROM complex_lists;
